steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence ends with "suggesting that ______" and must complete the logical argument. The passage says: most studies outside North America (e.g., Pyhtilä's Finland study) matched North American findings, while few matched Fagnani's Brazil study. The word "However" signals the hypothesis that the correlation is North-America-specific is being challenged. The blank must state the conclusion that follows.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "dissolved organic carbon and mercury levels do typically rise and fall together in fresh water.": This is supported by the evidence — most studies worldwide found the positive association, so it appears to be a general pattern, not a regional one. ✓
  • "there were circumstances unique to Pyhtilā and colleagues’ study that impeded accurate measurements of mercury levels.": The passage presents Pyhtilä's study as supporting the general association, not as flawed. This introduces an unsupported attack on that study. ✗
  • "the hypothesis that the positive association is particular to North America is correct.": This contradicts the passage. The "However" clause and the evidence from non-North American studies undermine this hypothesis. ✗
  • "levels of dissolved organic carbon and mercury in bodies of fresh water are both much higher in Brazil than elsewhere.": No information about absolute levels is given in the passage; this is unsupported. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
